message:
feat(combinator): implement hedge/retry futures and fix bulkhead queue leak

Completes the resilience combinator suite with real implementations of
hedge and retry, and fixes a correctness bug in bulkhead:

- bulkhead.rs: Fix cancel_entry() queue leak — previously, cancelling a
  pending or timed-out entry mutated it in place but left the zombie entry
  in the queue vector, causing unbounded memory growth. Now uses
  queue.remove(idx) unconditionally at the start of cancellation.

- hedge.rs: Implement the previously-stubbed hedge! macro and HedgeFuture.
  Polls the primary future, starts a deadline timer, spawns a backup via
  factory closure when elapsed, and races both completions.

- retry.rs: Implement the previously-stubbed retry! macro and Retry struct
  with Idle/Sleeping/Polling state machine supporting inter-attempt delays
  via Sleep, cancellation via Cx, and configurable retry predicates.

- rate_limit.rs: Replace fixed-point integer token bucket (u64 with
  FIXED_POINT_SCALE=1000) with direct f64 representation. Eliminates
  precision-loss starvation at very slow refill rates where integer
  truncation prevented tokens from ever accumulating.

- mod.rs: Export new HedgeFuture, hedge, Retry, and retry symbols.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
